name: What should I do differently with long-tail voice search keywords if I’ve already had a bad experience with an SEO company?

mainEntity.name: What should I do differently with long-tail voice search keywords if I’ve already had a bad experience with an SEO company?

acceptedAnswer.text:
If you’ve already worked with an SEO vendor and got disappointing results, start by auditing their approach: did they target real questions, provide clear answers, and measure outcomes beyond rankings? If not, you may need to reset expectations and strategy rather than abandon voice long-tail work entirely.

Past SEO issues often stem from overemphasis on vanity keywords instead of useful, question-based content.

KEY FACTORS
- Examine whether they focused on head terms over long-tail questions
- Check if content reads naturally and answers queries directly
- Look for missing schema, weak local signals, or poor mobile performance
- Clarify goals: calls, bookings, or revenue—not just rankings

Use the audit to define what “good” looks like for you: conversational content, clear answers, structured data, and measurable business outcomes. Then decide whether to retrain your current vendor, bring the work in-house, or switch partners. 

A refined, question-led voice strategy can succeed even after a prior SEO disappointment.
acceptedAnswer.description: Audit the past work: Did they target real questions, write natural answers, use schema, and track calls or bookings—not just rankings? Reset your strategy around conversational, question-based content tied to business outcomes, then decide whether to retrain that vendor, move the work in-house, or switch partners.
